H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78, confidence=strong. Largest n in dataset; strong DESCENT efficacy for over-stimulation. Qualitative: 66% positive (Noticeable=12, Slight=7). Settle_time ~10 min (11). Rhythm balanced (10) or calming (8). YouTube=30 (highest HEALING) but retention NULL. AutoGluon predicts 6.86 — close to observed.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: Most-used over-stimulation protocol with strong results — many felt noticeably better within 10 minutes. Balanced rhythm works well.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73, confidence=strong. VOL001 replicates strong pattern. Qualitative: Noticeable=10 dominates. Settle_time at 10 min (10). Music opinion more neutral (6) than L1. Rhythm calming (7 balanced 7). YouTube=5 — low discoverability.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Helped many with overstimulation, though some found it less effective than expected. Settling takes around 10 minutes.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful. Fastest settling in OS triad (~5 min=7). Qualitative: Noticeable=10, Moderate=4. Music Liked (11) vs Neutral (5). Rhythm balanced (10). YouTube=8. Drift at sim=0.784.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Reliable for unwinding, especially when trying to sleep. Faster settling than other overload options, with a steady rhythm.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93, confidence=strong. Single-volume data (VOL001) confirms strong DESCENT efficacy for anxiety. Qualitative: 63% positive (Moderate Improvement=10, Noticeable=8); settle_time peaks at 10 min (9 responses). AutoGluon predicts 7.84 — observed slightly underperforms. Rhythm feedback balanced/calming (17 of 23). YouTube views=7 with NULL retention — undiscovered reach potential.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Listeners found this anxiety protocol very settling — most felt moderate to significant improvement within about 10 minutes. The rhythm feels steady and calming.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful. VOL001 shows consistent anxiety support. Qualitative split: Noticeable=6, Moderate=6, Slight=5 — more mid-range than L1. Music opinion divided (Liked=9, Neutral=5). Settle_time ~5 min. Drift noted (sim=0.652) suggesting evolving analytical interpretation. No YouTube retention data.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: This anxiety protocol helps many feel better, though results are more mixed than with other anxiety options. Some found it calming, others were neutral.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89, confidence=meaningful. VOL001 completes anxiety triad. Qualitative: Noticeable=8, Moderate=7 — strong improvement reports. Settle_time longest in series (10 min=10, near end=4). Rhythm perceived as Very settling (10 of 20). Drift at sim=0.695 indicates analytical volatility. No retention data.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: A solid choice for anxiety that takes longer to settle in but has a very grounding rhythm most people appreciate.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful. Crisis protocol shows expected low mean given acute listener state. Qualitative: 53% positive with Significant=8, Noticeable=7 — higher efficacy than mean suggests. Settle_time fast (5 min=8, 10 min=5). Rhythm balanced (11) or calming (6). YouTube=6 views with no retention.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: Crisis protocol working as intended — listeners in acute distress often feel significant calm, with fast 5-10 minute settlement for most.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: n=33, mean=6.52, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful. Slightly higher than L1, maintaining crisis protocol pattern. Qualitative: Moderate=6, Noticeable=5, Significant=4 — improvement across spectrum. Music opinion split (Neutral=6, Loved=5). Settle_time ~10 min. Drift at sim=0.662.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: This crisis support helps during intense moments; the rhythm feels deeply soothing to many, with noticeable improvement for most.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91, confidence=meaningful. Fastest crisis settling (Immediate=7). Qualitative: mixed results but Noticeable=6. Music reception divided (Liked=7, Not liked=4). Rhythm balanced (12). Only 5 YouTube views — minimal reach.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Fastest crisis protocol with some immediate relief, though results vary more than other crisis options. Mixed feelings on the music.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: n=54, mean=7.19, std=1.86, confidence=strong. Leading sleep protocol. Qualitative: Noticeable=6, Moderate=5, Significant=3. Settle_time fast (5 min=5, Immediate=3). Rhythm balanced (7), calming (4). AutoGluon predicts 7.54 — near observed. YouTube=4.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Popular sleep protocol with fast-acting effects — many fell asleep within 5 minutes. Works well in therapy and study contexts.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55, confidence=meaningful. Unique listener mix: clinicians=6, caregivers=5. Qualitative: Noticeable=6, Slight=4. Music neutral (6). Rhythm too busy (3). Settle_time slower (10-20 min). YouTube=5. Drift at sim=0.802.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Sleep support with mixed results — some found it too rhythmically busy, others slept well. Slower to work (10-20 minutes).

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: n=28, mean=7.07, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful. Similar to L1/L2. Qualitative: Noticeable=7, Moderate=5, Significant=5. Settle_time fast (~5-10 min). Music split (Liked=8, Neutral=6). Rhythm slightly busy (7). YouTube=3.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Good for sleep and rest with moderate effectiveness. Faster settling than L2, though some found the rhythm slightly busy.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82, confidence=meaningful. Lower than expected for focus. Qualitative: Noticeable=10, Significant=6. Rhythm slightly busy (9). Settle_time fast (immediate-5 min). AutoGluon predicts L3 as best (7.73). YouTube=5.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Focus protocol with middle-ground results — works well but 9 people found the rhythm slightly busy, limiting effectiveness.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.60, confidence=meaningful. Strong shift from L1. Qualitative: Moderate=5, Significant=5. Settle_time ~10 min. Music neutral (6). Rhythm slightly busy (5). YouTube=4.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Stronger focus option with 66% positive results. Mixed opinions on music but generally moderate improvement.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97, confidence=meaningful. Highest THRIVING score. Qualitative: Significant=11, Noticeable=7 (bimodal: 11 significant vs 5 no effect). Music liked (12). Rhythm balanced (15). AutoGluon confirms 7.73 prediction. YouTube=15 (highest THRIVING).
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: Top-performing focus protocol — 71% felt positive effects with significant improvement reported most often. Well-liked music.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73, confidence=meaningful. Creative Flow HOLD protocol. Qualitative: Slight=8 dominates (less improvement than other CF). Settle_time immediate (6). Music liked (9). Rhythm balanced (9). AutoGluon does not prioritize. YouTube=2.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Creative flow support with modest results — many only felt slight improvement, though the rhythm is balanced and settling is fast.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41, confidence=meaningful. Improved over L1. Qualitative: Significant=5, Moderate=4. Settle_time ~10 min. Music Loved (6). Rhythm balanced (6). YouTube=2.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: This creative protocol helped several achieve significant improvement, with most loving the music. Settling takes about 10 minutes.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32, confidence=developing. Highest CF score at small n. Qualitative: Noticeable=7, Significant=6. Settle_time ~5-10 min. Music split (Neutral=7, Liked=9). Rhythm balanced (14). YouTube=7.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: Best creative flow option despite small data — many felt strong improvement with a balanced, well-received rhythm.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34, confidence=meaningful. Strong maintenance protocol. Qualitative: Moderate=6, Noticeable=5. Clinicians=6. Settle_time slowest (20 min=9). Rhythm balanced (12). YouTube=3.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Maintenance support with 64% positive outcomes — though settlement takes longest (around 20 minutes) in this category.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03, confidence=meaningful. Higher variance (std 2.03). Qualitative: Noticeable=5, Moderate=4. Settle_time ~5-10 min. Rhythm split (balanced/slightly busy 7 each). YouTube=4.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Balance protocol with moderate success — slightly higher variance than others but balanced rhythm overall.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73, confidence=developing. Small n but 71% positive. Qualitative: Noticeable=6, Moderate=4. Settle_time fast (5 min=8). 4 did NOT settle. Rhythm balanced (7). YouTube=3.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Small data but promising — 71% positive despite 4 listeners not settling. Balanced rhythm appreciated.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, confidence=meaningful. Mood/Energy ASCENT. AutoGluon predicts 8.23 — highest in dataset, but observed 6.74. Qualitative: Moderate=7, Significant=6. Settle_time slow (20 min=6). Cross-bucket routing to multiple zones suggests pan-protocol utility. YouTube=8.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Mood protocol with potential but modest results (6.74) — works slowly (20 min) but AutoGluon predicts high future efficacy.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, confidence=meaningful. Similar to L1. Qualitative: Significant=7 (strong), Moderate=4. Settle_time split. Music Loved (6). Highest drift (sim=0.532). YouTube=8.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Stronger mood support (64% positive) with faster settling for some. Music generally well-received.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, confidence=developing. Lowest ME score. 5 reported no effect. Settle_time ~5-10 min. Music liked (11). Rhythm balanced (9). High drift (sim=0.627). YouTube=4.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Lowest energy protocol with 5 no-effect reports — less consistent than L1/L2 despite pleasant music.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01, confidence=meaningful. Lowest MD positive rate (45%). Qualitative: Noticeable=6, Significant=5. Settle_time fast (~5 min=9). Rhythm balanced (7). AutoGluon predicts 7.88 — significant underperformance. YouTube=5.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Motivation protocol with lowest positive rate (45%) and high variance — some benefit, but inconsistent results overall.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63, confidence=development. Low positive rate (43%). Qualitative: mixed (Noticeable=6, Moderate=5, Slight=5). Settle_time slow (20 min=5). Music loved (9). Highest drift (sim=0.585). YouTube=7.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Similar to L1 with 43% positive — slower to work (20 min for some), though many loved the music.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, confidence=developing. Highest MD score — reverses L1/L2 pattern dramatically. Qualitative: Moderate=8 (dominant), Noticeable=5. Settle_time fast (5 min=5). Highest analytical drift (sim=0.507) — panel divided on interpretation. YouTube=7.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Highest motivation score despite small data — 62% felt positive, with moderate improvement most common. Fast settling.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful. Mind clarity DESCENT. Qualitative: Noticeable=10, Moderate=8 — 2 got worse. Music loved/liked (9 each). Rhythm calming (8). AutoGluon predicts L2 highest (7.54). YouTube=8.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Mind clarity protocol working well with 54% positive — though 2 listeners said it made things worse. Calming rhythm helps.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64, confidence=developing. Higher music appreciation (Loved=6). Settle_time split (immediate=6, 10 min=6). Rhythm very calming (12). YouTube=12 (highest X-MC).
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Most popular clarity option with strong 'very settling' rhythm feedback. 56% felt positive improvement.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45, confidence=developing. PRIMARY ANOMALY — largest negative autoGluon delta. Distribution: [0,0,1,1,2,1,0,3,0,2] shows bimodal subgroup effect. Rhythm too busy (4). Only 50% positive. Drift at sim=0.696.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: ⚠️ Mixed results with lowest score (5.7) — about half felt improvements, others found rhythm too busy. Needs investigation.

sphere_commentary.overview:
HEALING protocols lead in consistency while TRANSFORMING shows expected higher variance; multiple anomalies warrant investigation.
• HEALING achieves highest mean (6.98) with tightest std (1.77), confirming nervous system regulation design intent.
• THRIVING Mean=6.96 shows strong Focus protocol performance (T-FC-L3 mean=7.63) despite smaller n.
• TRANSFORMING shows widest spread (std=1.87) — expected for ascending-arc designs targeting low-affect states.
• Crisis protocols (H-CR-*) show structurally expected lower scores (6.35-6.52) given listener entry severity.
• X-MC-L3 emerges as primary anomaly: mean=5.7 vs predicted=7.54 (AutoGluon delta -1.84) suggests design mismatch.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
Series performance aligns with design intent; protocol type hierarchy preserved with DESCENT leading ASCENT.
• HEALING (DESCENT dominant): Consistent high scores 6.92-7.22 across subcategories; Over-stimulation protocols lead.
• THRIVING (HOLD dominant): Strong dose-response at L3 levels (T-FC-L3=7.63, T-MB-L3=7.04); L3 variants outperform L1 in all categories.
• TRANSFORMING (ASCENT/DESCENT mix): Highest variance (std=1.87); ASCENT protocols underperform DESCENT; Motivation triad shows dramatic internal divergence (X-MD-L1=6.36 to X-MD-L3=7.15).
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
Four substantive anomalies detected across datasets — three show panel convergence.
• X-MC-L3: Mean=5.7 vs AutoGluon predicted=7.54 (largest negative delta in dataset); bimodal distribution [0,0,1,1,2,1,0,3,0,2] suggests subgroup effect.
• T-FC-L3: Strong outcome (mean=7.63) with bimodal change_rating (Significant improvement=11, No effect=5); AutoGluon cross-buckets to X-ME-L1.
• H-OS-L1: Highest YouTube views (30) across all protocols but retention data NULL; outcome strong (mean=7.15) but reach limited.
• Panel divided on X-MD-L3: High mean (7.15) but highest embedding drift (sim=0.507) — unclear if signal or analytical instability.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Multiple actionable signals emerge; three show convergent panel agreement.
• [CONVERGENT] X-MC-L3: Investigate low efficacy — protocol shows 5.7 mean vs 7.54 predicted; create L2 variant adjusting Hz arc or remove from active rotation.
• [CONVERGENT] T-FC-L3: Prioritise replication in VOL002 — strong 7.63 mean with 71% positive; highest THRIVING score.
• [CONVERGENT] H-OS-L1: Increase discovery — strong efficacy (7.15) with highest HEALING views (30) but untapped potential; explore adviser partnerships.
• X-ME-L1: Investigate cross-bucket routing — AutoGluon predicts 8.23 for low-affect states but observed 6.74; upgrade to dual-entry status (ASCENT + crisis) or adjust hold phase.
• H-CR-L1: Extend HOLD phase per settle_time feedback (5min=8 responses); create L2 variant testing 15-minute duration.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Significant drift detected across protocols; semantic clustering insufficient data.
• CROSS-RUN DRIFT: 27 of 30 protocols show sim < 0.85 threshold, indicating analytical instability or evolving patterns — X-MD-L3 (0.507), X-ME-L2 (0.532), X-MD-L2 (0.585) show highest drift.
• CROSS-PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: Insufficient embeddings for clustering — multiple runs needed before reliable grouping.
• Confidence: Drift metrics reliable (n>20 analyses per protocol); cluster analysis confidence low pending embedding data accumulation.

by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L1: Listeners found this anxiety protocol very settling — most felt moderate to significant improvement within about 10 minutes. The rhythm feels steady and calming.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L2: This anxiety protocol helps many feel better, though results are more mixed than with other anxiety options. Some found it calming, others were neutral.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L3: A solid choice for anxiety that takes longer to settle in but has a very grounding rhythm most people appreciate.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L1: Crisis protocol working as intended — listeners in acute distress often feel significant calm, with fast 5-10 minute settlement for most.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L2: This crisis support helps during intense moments; the rhythm feels deeply soothing to many, with noticeable improvement for most.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L3: Fastest crisis protocol with some immediate relief, though results vary more than other crisis options. Mixed feelings on the music.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L1: Most-used over-stimulation protocol with strong results — many felt noticeably better within 10 minutes. Balanced rhythm works well.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L2: Helped many with overstimulation, though some found it less effective than expected. Settling takes around 10 minutes.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L3: Reliable for unwinding, especially when trying to sleep. Faster settling than other overload options, with a steady rhythm.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L1: Popular sleep protocol with fast-acting effects — many fell asleep within 5 minutes. Works well in therapy and study contexts.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L2: Sleep support with mixed results — some found it too rhythmically busy, others slept well. Slower to work (10-20 minutes).
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L3: Good for sleep and rest with moderate effectiveness. Faster settling than L2, though some found the rhythm slightly busy.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L1: Creative flow support with modest results — many only felt slight improvement, though the rhythm is balanced and settling is fast.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L2: This creative protocol helped several achieve significant improvement, with most loving the music. Settling takes about 10 minutes.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L3: Best creative flow option despite small data — many felt strong improvement with a balanced, well-received rhythm.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L1: Focus protocol with middle-ground results — works well but 9 people found the rhythm slightly busy, limiting effectiveness.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L2: Stronger focus option with 66% positive results. Mixed opinions on music but generally moderate improvement.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L3: Top-performing focus protocol — 71% felt positive effects with significant improvement reported most often. Well-liked music.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L1: Maintenance support with 64% positive outcomes — though settlement takes longest (around 20 minutes) in this category.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L2: Balance protocol with moderate success — slightly higher variance than others but balanced rhythm overall.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L3: Small data but promising — 71% positive despite 4 listeners not settling. Balanced rhythm appreciated.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L1: Mind clarity protocol working well with 54% positive — though 2 listeners said it made things worse. Calming rhythm helps.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L2: Most popular clarity option with strong 'very settling' rhythm feedback. 56% felt positive improvement.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L3: ⚠️ Mixed results with lowest score (5.7) — about half felt improvements, others found rhythm too busy. Needs investigation.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L1: Motivation protocol with lowest positive rate (45%) and high variance — some benefit, but inconsistent results overall.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L2: Similar to L1 with 43% positive — slower to work (20 min for some), though many loved the music.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L3: Highest motivation score despite small data — 62% felt positive, with moderate improvement most common. Fast settling.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L1: Mood protocol with potential but modest results (6.74) — works slowly (20 min) but AutoGluon predicts high future efficacy.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L2: Stronger mood support (64% positive) with faster settling for some. Music generally well-received.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L3: Lowest energy protocol with 5 no-effect reports — less consistent than L1/L2 despite pleasant music.
